object.description: An unordered collection of key-value pairs enclosed in curly braces. Keys must be double-quoted strings.

array.description: An ordered sequence of values enclosed in square brackets, separated by commas.

string.description: A sequence of Unicode characters enclosed in double quotes. Supports escape sequences like \n, \t, \u0000.

number.description: A numeric value - integer or floating point. No leading zeros, no hex/octal. Supports scientific notation.

boolean.description: A boolean literal, either true or false (lowercase only).

null.description: Represents the absence of a value. Must be lowercase.

key.description: Object keys must be double-quoted strings. Each key in an object should be unique.

escape_sequences.description: Special characters in strings are represented with backslash escape sequences.

json_schema.description: JSON Schema is a vocabulary for annotating and validating JSON documents. The $schema keyword declares which version of the JSON Schema spec the document uses.

json_pointer.description: JSON Pointer (RFC 6901) is a syntax for identifying a specific value within a JSON document using a path of property names and array indices.

mime_type.description: The official MIME type for JSON is application/json. The file extension is .json.

trailing_comma.description: Trailing commas are NOT allowed in standard JSON. This is a common error when editing JSON files.

comments.description: Standard JSON does not support comments. Some tools (JSONC, JSON5) extend JSON to allow them. Use _comment keys as a workaround.
